purpose: Generate pronounceable nonsense wrappers for induction / updating / rival-rule tracking items while preserving underlying logical form.

foil_rules: The stream repeats each state twice before changing.
The stream cycles through three states.
The stream stays on the same state for two steps, then changes.

surface_rules: The stream cycles through {A}, {B}, and {C} in order.
After {A} comes {B}, after {B} comes {C}, and after {C} comes {A}.
The state moves one step up the ladder each time.
The state moves two steps up the ladder each time.
ladder_rule.ladder_requirement: Premises must define the ordered ladder explicitly, e.g. Lur < Mav < Nef < Ralo < Tiven.

surface_rules: Each state repeats twice before the sequence advances.
The stream moves in two-item blocks.
One feature cycles while the second feature stays fixed.
The first feature changes systematically but the second stays constant.

relation_templates.query_template: Which rule best fits the observations so far?
relation_templates.good_option_rule: Select the most specific candidate rule still strongly supported by the observations.

relation_templates.query_template: Should confidence in the current best rule rise relative to the rival?
relation_templates.good_option_rule: The new observation must favour the named current best rule more than the rival rule.

relation_templates.query_template: Select all candidate rules that are still consistent with the observations.
relation_templates.good_option_rule: Correct options can include both a specific rule and a broader super-rule when both remain live.
foil_rules: feature-change claim contradicted by fixed feature

binding_load.1: single one-dimensional pattern over one feature
binding_load.2: three-state pattern or multiple live rival rules
binding_load.3: two-feature pattern or multi-step confidence update
binding_load.5: high-load mixed-feature or noisy update item

uncertainty_level.2: one best rule with one plausible rival
uncertainty_level.4: ambiguous or noisy evidence requiring calibrated updating

control_burden.3: multiple close rivals or wrapper shift
control_burden.4: multi-select, feature interference, or fast decisions

tier_progression.description: Simple one-dimensional alternation or +1 step rule. One clearly best answer.
Cycle-of-3 or +2 step rule. More plausible distractors.
Pair-repeat or longer cycle. More rival rules and stronger near-miss foils.
Two-feature rule where one feature changes and the other stays fixed. Confidence-update items become common.
High-load mixed-feature or noisy evidence items with multi-select filtering and higher control burden.

complexity_rules.expansion_note: Later versions can add causal-model update, matrix-rule candidates, and verbal-category induction variants.

adaptive_rules.decision_logic: Use block-level UP / HOLD / DOWN with the same principle as the integrated game: stabilise first, then wrapper swap, then speed, then load.

routing.high_alternation_confusion: return to tier 1-2 one-dimensional alternation blocks
routing.high_wrapper_cost: hold tier and keep real-world wrapper before pushing nonsense
